sprint: Change Look Left Stale Object Strip Content From the Previous Scene
task: Real, user-reported bug found authoring a Landscape Dual Holder Scene in World Builder v2's test theme: switching Page Style via Change Look showed a Decoration Shape from the PREVIOUS, unrelated Scene bleeding onto the new page, and Artwork Place was missing. Root cause, confirmed via a real headless-Build + real-import reproduction: js/creationFlow.js's _applyRepresentationToCurrentSlide() correctly updated slide.metadata.layout and called redrawPreview (canvas geometry/wall tone were already correct, confirmed via pixel sampling), but predates the Creator Page Runtime Sprint's PageRuntime.notify() dispatch and never called ObjectStrip.refresh() -- so Object Strip kept showing stale Layer Pack objects from the previous Representation's Scene. Fixed by calling PageRuntime.notify() (with a fallback including the previously-missing ObjectStrip.refresh() if PageRuntime isn't loaded). Two things investigated and confirmed NOT bugs, disclosed rather than silently fixed: the whole page canvas staying portrait-shaped regardless of a Scene's Aspect Ratio is Engine V1's known, disclosed, fixed-1080x1350-canvas limitation (the picture Frame area inside it does correctly resolve to the Scene's own aspect); the wall-tone colour filling the entire page behind a smaller picture placeholder is Sprint 9.7's own deliberate wall-level design, not a rendering bug. A separate, disclosed, structural platform ceiling, not touched this session: Engine V1 only ever converges the FIRST Place's Frame per Scene (its own code comment discloses this) -- a Dual/Triple/Grid Holder Scene can only ever offer ONE artwork upload area in Creator; lifting that is a real architecture change needing its own approval. Verified via a new Playwright reproduction building a real two-Scene World through the real ProjectCompiler, importing the real compiled package, and driving the real Change Look UI flow, plus a pixel-sampling A/B isolating the fix to Object Strip staleness alone. Full regression across the entire existing Creator/Builder/Card-Platform Playwright suite (22 scripts) and both goldenBuild.js suites passes unchanged, zero console errors. See CLAUDE.md's matching entry for the full account.
